# Hantavirus Monitor

> Live global hantavirus surveillance dashboard — confirmed cases, clusters, deaths, and environmental advisories for HPS (Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome), HFRS (Hemorrhagic Fever with Renal Syndrome), Andes virus, Sin Nombre virus, Hantaan, Puumala, Seoul, and Choclo strains.

The Hantavirus Monitor (`bookhockeys.com/hantavirus`) is a single-file static SPA hosted on GitHub Pages from
[github.com/itsavibecode/book/tree/main/hantavirus](https://github.com/itsavibecode/book/tree/main/hantavirus).
No backend; no upload of user content; no signup. The page reads a curated event dataset and renders it as a war-monitor-style live operations dashboard.

## What it shows

- **World map** (Leaflet + CARTO Dark) with severity-coloured pins per event.
- **Live wire feed** — sortable, filterable list of events with severity badges and category chips.
- **Top stat strip** — cases (90d), deaths (18mo), most active region, dominant strain.
- **Scrolling LIVE ticker** — eight most recent events, with user-controllable scroll speed.
- **Timeline panel** — 16-month horizontal strip with one severity-coloured dot per event.
- **Social tab** — official X/Twitter embeds curated from authoritative public-health accounts: CDC (@CDCgov), WHO (@WHO), the WHO Director-General (@DrTedros), and the Outbreak Updates aggregator (@outbreakupdates). PAHO, ECDC, NM Department of Health, and Colorado DPH are queued as awaiting feeds.

## Severity model

- **S1** — confirmed case, recovered.
- **S2** — confirmed case, hospitalised or active.
- **S3** — death, or cluster of three or more linked cases.

Pin colours match severity: cyan = S1, yellow = S2, red = S3 (with a pulsing animation).

## Categories

`CASE`, `DEATH`, `CLUSTER`, `ADVISORY`, `ENV` (environmental, e.g. NPS rodent advisories), plus strain tags `HPS`, `HFRS`, `ANDES`, `HANTAAN`, `PUUMALA`, `SEOUL`, `CHOCLO`.

## Data sources (current state)

The current event set is a **curated mix**: documented public events (Mono County 2025 cluster, Santa Fe County 2025 fatality) plus representative endemic-surveillance entries that match real ongoing patterns (Hantaan in Shaanxi/Heilongjiang, Puumala in Bashkortostan and Finland, Andes in Patagonia, Choclo in Panama). The dashboard's footer and an in-page banner label this clearly. Authoritative live sources should be checked at `cdc.gov`, `who.int`, and the relevant state or national health departments.

## Data ingest plan (v0.2+)

A Cloudflare Worker on a nightly cron will ingest:

1. State / national DOH RSS feeds (NM, CA, AZ, CO, UT, WA in the US; provincial CDCs in CN/AR/CL/RU/FI/KR).
2. Google News RSS for `hantavirus`, `HPS`, `HFRS` keyword searches.
3. NPS rodent-advisory RSS feeds.
4. Curated X-handle posts via Nitter RSS, with a fallback list of mirror instances.

The Worker writes normalised event records to Firestore; the SPA reads them at page-load. A small admin route lets the curator promote, edit, or dismiss auto-ingested items.

## Privacy

No image data, account data, or personally-identifying information is collected by the dashboard. Google Analytics 4 (`G-DYME377V2S`, shared with the rest of `bookhockeys.com`) records page views and a few anonymous events. Visitors with a European (`Europe/*`) timezone — plus EEA outliers like Iceland, Cyprus, and Atlantic-island EU territories — see a Google Consent Mode v2 banner on first visit and analytics is denied by default until they choose; visitors elsewhere have analytics enabled by default with no banner. Choice is persisted in `localStorage` under `greenline-consent` (shared site-wide with the rest of bookhockeys.com).

X / Twitter embeds load `platform.twitter.com/widgets.js` lazily — only when the SOCIAL tab is opened **and** at least one curated tweet has a real ID.

## Known limits

- Snapshot data only. The dashboard is for situational awareness; clinical and public-health decisions should be made against authoritative sources.
- The 18-month death window mid-rolls; a slow news year reads as a quiet dashboard. That is correct, not broken.
- Nitter availability is volatile; the v0.2 ingest pipeline assumes occasional fallback to Google News RSS.
